quality.rationale: 基于宇宙学流体四速度场分解，需计算克里斯托费尔符号与投影张量并多步推导膨胀标量、旋率与剪切张量

origin.question: [文件 1: 1_s19_19.40.md]Problem 19.40. For the congruence of galaxy world lines in a Robertson-Walker cosmology find $\theta, \omega_{\alpha \beta}$ and $\sigma_{\alpha \beta}$ . (See Problem 5.18 for definitions.) Do the same for the anisotropic metric$$\mathrm {d s}^{2} = - \mathrm {d t}^{2} + \mathrm {e}^{2 a} \mathrm {d x}^{2} + \mathrm {e}^{2 b} \mathrm {d y}^{2} + \mathrm {e}^{2 c} \mathrm {d z}^{2}$$where a, b, and c are functions of t and where x, y and z are coordinates comoving with the galaxies.## Solution
origin.answer: Robertson-Walker: $\theta = 3\dot{R}/R$, $\omega_{\alpha\beta}=0$, $\sigma_{\alpha\beta}=0$. Anisotropic metric: $\theta = \dot{a}+\dot{b}+\dot{c}$, $\omega_{\alpha\beta}=0$, $\sigma_{ij}=\frac{1}{3}Ag_{ij}$ with $A=\{2\dot{a}-\dot{b}-\dot{c}, 2\dot{b}-\dot{a}-\dot{c}, 2\dot{c}-\dot{a}-\dot{b}\}$ for $x,y,z$ respectively.
origin.solution: Solution 19.40. Since $\mathbf{u} = \mathbf{e}_0$ we have$$\mathrm {u}_{\alpha ; \beta} = \mathrm {u}_{\alpha , \beta} - \Gamma^{\gamma}_{\alpha \beta} \mathrm {u}_{\gamma} = 0 + \Gamma^{0}_{\alpha \beta}$$so that$$\theta = \mathrm {u}_{\alpha ; \beta} \mathrm {g}^{\alpha \beta} = \Gamma_{\alpha \beta}^{0} \mathrm {g}^{\alpha \beta} = - (- \mathrm {g})^{- \frac {1}{2}} [ \mathrm {g}^{0 \alpha} (- \mathrm {g})^{\frac {1}{2}} ], a \tag {1}$$(see part (f) of Problem 7.7). Hence for a Robertson-Walker cosmology$$\theta = (- \mathbf {g})^{- \frac {1}{2}} (- \mathbf {g})_{, 0}^{\frac {1}{2}} = 3 \dot {\mathbf {R}} / \mathbf {R}.$$The rotation is$$\omega_{\alpha \beta} = \mathrm {P}_{\beta}^{\gamma} \mathrm {u}_{[ a; \gamma ]} = 0$$since $\Gamma_{\alpha \beta}^{0}$ is symmetric. For the shear we have$$\sigma_{\alpha \beta} = \mathrm {P}_{\beta}^{\gamma} \mathrm {u}_{(\alpha ; \gamma)} - \frac {1}{3} \theta \mathrm {P}_{\alpha \beta}$$$$\sigma_{0 a} = 0$$$$\begin{array}{l} \sigma_{i j} = u_{(i, j)} - \frac {1}{3} \theta g_{i j} \\\ = - \frac {1}{2} \left(\mathrm {g}_{0 \mathrm {i}, \mathrm {j}} + \mathrm {g}_{0 \mathrm {j}, \mathrm {i}} - \mathrm {g}_{\mathrm {i j}, 0}\right) - \frac {\dot {\mathrm {R}}}{\mathrm {R}} \mathrm {g}_{\mathrm {i j}} \\\ = \frac {1}{2} g_{i j, 0} - \dot {R} / R g_{i j} = 0 \\ \end{array}$$For the anisotropic metric, Equation (1) still holds, so$$\begin{array}{l} \theta = (- \mathrm {g})^{- \frac {1}{2}} (- \mathrm {g})_{, 0}^{\frac {1}{2}} = \dot {\mathrm {a}} + \dot {\mathrm {b}} + \dot {\mathrm {c}} \\\ \omega_{\alpha \beta} = 0 \\\ \sigma_{0 \alpha} = 0 \\\ \sigma_{i j} = \frac {1}{2} g_{i j, 0} - \frac {1}{3} \theta g_{i j} = \frac {1}{3} A g_{i j} \\ \end{array}$$where$$A = \left\{\begin{array}{l l} 2 \dot {a} - \dot {b} - \dot {c} & \text {f o r} \quad i = j = x \\\ 2 \dot {b} - \dot {a} - \dot {c} & \text {f o r} \quad i = j = y \\\ 2 \dot {c} - \dot {a} - \dot {b} & \text {f o r} \quad i = j = z. \end{array}\right.$$
